title: The Song of Amergin
source: Lebor Gabala Erenn; Book of Leinster
description: The oldest poem in Irish literature: the druid Amergin claims identity with every element of creation.
introduction: The Song of Amergin is attributed to the first Gaelic poet to set foot on Irish soil. In it, the druid-poet claims identity with every force of nature — wind, wave, hawk, dewdrop, salmon, hill — in a litany that scholars compare to Hindu and Egyptian 'I am' declarations. It may preserve the oldest stratum of Celtic cosmological thought.

translation.original_text: Am gaeth i m-muir. Am tond trethan. Am fuaim mara.
translation.text: I am wind on sea. I am ocean wave. I am roar of sea.

translation.original_text: Am dam secht ndirend. Am loch i m-maigh. Am bri danae.
translation.text: I am a stag of seven tines. I am a lake on a plain. I am a hill of poetry.
